Richard Cater-Carter of Dover, New Hampshire and Some of His Descendants, by Robert Givens (1972), Chapter 5 (The Life of Richard IV Carter and Sarah Peavey) -
     Richard IV grew up on what was lef …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Carter於大約1694年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓時，他的父親Richard Carter III22歲，母親Elizabeth22歲。他於1719年2月2日與Sarah Peavey在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾聶文頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1753年5月25日在美國緬因州波藍德斯卡波洛享年60歲，埋葬在美國緬因州波藍德斯卡波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Carter于大约1694年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓时，他的父亲Richard Carter III22岁，母亲Elizabeth22岁。他于1719年2月2日与Sarah Peavey在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾聶文頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1753年5月25日在美国缅因州波藍德斯卡波洛享年60岁，埋葬在美国缅因州波藍德斯卡波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Carter was born about 1694, in Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, his father, Richard Carter III, was 22 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 22. He married Sarah Peavey on 2 February 1719, in Newington, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 25 May 1753, in Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, United States, at the age of 60, and was buried in Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Carter est né environ 1694, à Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Richard Carter III, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Peavey le 2 février 1719, à Newington, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 25 mai 1753, à Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Carter um 1694 in Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Carter III 22 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 22. Er hat am 2. Februar 1719 in Newington, New Hampshire, British Colonial America Sarah Peavey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Mai 1753 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Carter è nato il circa 1694 nel Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Richard Carter III, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Peavey il 2 febbraio 1719, nel Newington, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 25 maggio 1753, nel Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Carterが約1694年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムニューイントンで生まれた時，父Richard Carter IIIは22歳，母Elizabethは22歳であった。1719年2月2日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアニューイントンでSarah Peaveyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1753年5月25日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州クウンバーランドスカーボロウで60歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国メイン州クウンバーランドスカーボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.Greenland, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 그린랜드

ko.Scarborough, Cumberland, Massachusetts B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 요르크실르 스카버루그h
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Carter(이)가 대략 1694년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 뉴잉턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Carter III(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1719년 2월 2일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 뉴잉턴에서 Sarah Peavey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1753년 5월 25일 미국 메인 주 c벌랜드 스카버루그h에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 c벌랜드 스카버루그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Carter nasceu aproximadamente 1694, em Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, seu pai, Richard Carter III, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Peavey em 2 de fevereiro de 1719, em Newington, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de maio de 1753, em Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Carter родился около 1694 г. в местности нюингтон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Richard Carter III было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Peavey 2 февраля 1719 г. в местности нюингтон, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 25 мая 1753 г. в местности скарбороую, кумбирланд, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности скарбороую, кумбирланд, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Carter nació aproximadamente 1694, en Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Carter III, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Sarah Peavey el 2 de febrero de 1719, en Newington, New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 25 de mayo de 1753, en Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Scarborough, Cumberland, Maine, Estados Unidos.
